chunks.page_summary: This page provides a step-by-step guide for integrating Rheonics inline viscometers and density sensors with Modicon M262 PLCs using an EDS file and Machine Expert Logic builder software.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Ethernet/IP integration with Modicon M262

Modified on: Thu, 9 Mar, 2023 at 3:38 AM

Rheonics sensors are compatible with Modicon M262 PLC, for the correct integration of our sensor follow these steps:

1. Install the EDS file

1.1. Go to the support portal and download the eds file that corresponds to your sensor.
EthernetIP EDS file for Rheonics inline viscometer and density meter
(https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000393236)

1.2. Open Machine Expert Logic builder

1.3. Go to the "tools" tab and select Device Repository

[Image: Screenshot of Machine Expert Logic Builder with 'Tools' menu open and 'Device Repository' highlighted]

Figure 1. Selection of Device Repository option on Tools tab

1.4. Click the Install button and select the eds file that is downloaded from the Rheonics Support Portal, once the file is installed it will appear under the option of "Ethernet/Ip targets/Other"
key_facts: Rheonics sensors are compatible with Modicon M262 PLC.
An EDS file is required for the correct integration of Rheonics sensors with Modicon M262 PLC.
The EDS file can be downloaded from the Rheonics Support Portal.
The integration process involves using Machine Expert Logic builder software.
After installation, the EDS file will appear under "Ethernet/Ip targets/Other" in the software.
diagram_labels: Figure 1. Selection of Device Repository option on Tools tab

chunks.page_summary: This page details the process of installing an EDS file for a Rheonics SRV sensor into a device repository and outlines the initial steps for adding the sensor to a project within a software environment like EcoStruxure Machine Expert.

Figure 2. Installing eds file for SRV sensor.
2. Adding Rheonics Sensor to Project
2.1. To add the Rheonics Sensor to your project, add the controller that matches your requirement (Modicon M262) and select Ethernet port "Ethernet_2(ETH2)". Click the green plus symbol.
key_facts: The page illustrates the installation of an EDS (Electronic Data Sheet) file for a Rheonics SRV sensor.
The SRV sensor is listed with Rheonics as its vendor and has a Major Revision of 16#1 and Minor Revision of 16#1.
The installation process involves using a 'Device Repository' tool, likely within 'EcoStruxure Machine Expert'.
Adding the Rheonics Sensor to a project requires selecting a compatible controller, such as Modicon M262, and a specific Ethernet port labeled 'Ethernet_2(ETH2)'.
The final step for adding the sensor involves clicking a 'green plus symbol' in the software interface.
diagram_labels: String for a full text search

2.3. Right-click into the Ethernet/IP scanner to insert a new device, and write “SRV” on the search bar and the sensor to your project.
key_facts: The document demonstrates configuration steps within the Machine Expert Logic Builder software, version 2.0.3 (x64).
The first step involves selecting the Ethernet_2 (ETH2) port under 'MyController (TM262L01MESE8T)' in the 'Devices tree'.
The second step is to add the 'EtherNet/IP Scanner' from the 'Protocol Managers' folder within the 'Add Device' dialog.
The 'EtherNet/IP Scanner', provided by Schneider Electric with version 1.0.0.23, is a service designed to manage the Industrial Network and includes a scanner for device monitoring and controlling.
The third step instructs the user to right-click the Ethernet/IP scanner, insert a new device, and search for 'SRV' to add a specific sensor to the project.
diagram_labels: Figure 3. Selecting the Ethernet port to establish the Ethernet/IP Scanner
2.2. Add Ethernet/IP Scanner under Protocol Managers folder.
String for a full text search
This service allows to manage the Industr (description snippet)
This service allows to create his own prot: (description snippet)
Description: This service allows to manage the Industrial Network. It embeds an scanner for device monitoring and controlling. (details pane)
Figure 4. Adding Ethernet/IP scanner to the project.
2.3. Right-click into the Ethernet/IP scanner to insert a new device, and write “SRV” on the search bar and the sensor to your project.

key_facts: The Rheonics SRV device is an EtherNet/IP Remote Adapter.
The configuration process involves adding the SRV device to the device tree, assigning its IP address, and verifying electronic keying information.
The Electronic Keying parameters (Device Type, Vendor Code, Product Code, Major Revision, Minor Revision) are used for device verification.
EtherNet/IP is the communication protocol between the logic controller and the SRV device.
The Requested Packet Interval (RPI) for Scanner to Target (Output) communication is configured to 3200 milliseconds.
The Requested Packet Interval (RPI) for Target to Scanner (Input) communication is configured to 10 milliseconds, with an input size of 373 bytes.
diagram_labels: String for a full text search
EtherNet/IP Target imported from EDS File: RheonicsSRV.eds
This is the protocol used between the logic controller and the device.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides a step-by-step guide for configuring an SRV sensor within the Machine Expert Logic Builder software, focusing on I/O mapping and PLC settings.

key_facts: To configure I/O mappings for the SRV sensor, navigate to the 'Ethernet I/O Mapping' tab, right-click the SRV sensor symbol, and select 'Import mappings from CSV'.
The CSV file containing SRV mappings should be downloaded from the current page.
It is recommended to set the 'Always update variables' parameter to 'Enabled 2 (always in bus cycle task)' for optimal performance.
The software used is 'Machine Expert Logic Builder' version 2.0.3 (x64).
diagram_labels: Disabled (Update only if used in task)
Enabled 1 (use bus cycle task if not used in any task)
Generate force variables for IO mapping
warnings_notes: It is recommended to set the parameter 'Always update variables' on the value 'always in bus cycles task'.
